subject: DevOps
title: AWS us-east-1 S3 Outage (2017)
description: A 60-second lesson on AWS us-east-1 S3 Outage (2017).

hook.text: February 28, 2017. A typo in an AWS command took down S3. Half the internet broke.

buildup.text: An engineer ran a debug command to remove a few S3 servers. A typo removed far too many.

discovery.text: S3 in us-east-1 went down. Thousands of websites, apps, and IoT devices failed instantly.

twist.text: Even the AWS status dashboard was hosted on S3. It showed green while the world burned.

climax.text: AWS added safeguards: slower removal rates and minimum capacity thresholds. One typo changed policy.

punchline.text: Guard against fat-finger errors. Rate-limit destructive ops.

quiz.question: What caused the 2017 AWS S3 outage?
options: A typo in a command removed too many servers
A natural disaster at the data center
